findings.message: Two bash execution steps reference a hardcoded developer-local absolute path. The steps will fail silently on every non-developer installation with a directory-not-found error.
Hardcoded developer home directory leaks contributor username and local filesystem layout to anyone who reads the agent definition file.

All 5 agent files use 3–5 vague quantifiers (comprehensive, seamless, effective, thorough) without measurable thresholds. The phrasing adds length without adding precision about what the agent delivers. Representative file shown.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.

11 of 11 slash commands with valid frontmatter omit the `allowed-tools` field. Without this field Claude Code cannot enforce tool boundaries. Affected files: ci-guard, sync-branch, run-release, sync-todos-to-github, review, security-scan, git/rv, git/cp, git/sc, git/cm, git/pr. Representative file shown.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not be registered by Claude Code despite containing well-documented content.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.

Uses positional shell variables $1/$2 to parse slash command arguments. Claude Code supplies arguments via $ARGUMENTS only; at runtime $1 and $2 expand to empty strings, silently producing a GitHub issue with an empty title.
No YAML frontmatter block; the `description` field required for slash command registration is absent. Claude Code will not register this command.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register.

The generated-skills/CLAUDE.md catalog is out of sync with the repository: 5 confirmed skills are present on disk but absent from the 'Available Skills' section. Users browsing the catalog to discover skills will miss roughly one-third of the available catalog.
Pre-scan matched subprocess.run pattern. Investigation confirms the call uses list-form argument with shell=False (default). No injection surface exists.

Makes outbound HTTP POST requests to Slack and Microsoft Teams webhook URLs via urllib.request.urlopen(). URLs are read from SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL / TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL env vars or a config file. Feature is opt-in and documented but is not disclosed in SKILL.md and lacks timeout or error-handling guards.

High-density vague quantifiers across all 14 skill definitions: 'comprehensive', 'intelligent', 'seamless', 'advanced', 'actionable', 'data-driven' used without measurable criteria. Worst offender: tech-stack-evaluator (8 instances). Systematic output of the factory generation template. Representative file shown.

No YAML frontmatter block; the `description` field required for slash command registration is absent. Claude Code will not register this command. — File begins with prose content; no --- delimiters present at document start; grep finds zero YAML frontmatter blocks.

No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not be registered by Claude Code despite containing well-documented content. — File begins with prose content; no --- delimiters present at document start.
No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register. — File begins with prose content; no --- delimiters present at document start.

No YAML frontmatter block; `description` field absent; command will not register. — File begins with prose content; no --- delimiters present at document start.

Two bash execution steps reference a hardcoded developer-local absolute path. The steps will fail silently on every non-developer installation with a directory-not-found error. — Line 253: `cd /Users/rezarezvani/projects/claude-code-skills-factory && ...`; same path at line 259. Path is developer-specific and does not exist on any other machine.

Makes outbound HTTP POST requests to Slack and Microsoft Teams webhook URLs via urllib.request.urlopen(). URLs are read from SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL / TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL env vars or a config file. Feature is opt-in and documented but is not disclosed in SKILL.md and lacks timeout or error-handling guards.

Hardcoded developer home directory leaks contributor username and local filesystem layout to anyone who reads the agent definition file. — Line 253 contains `/Users/rezarezvani/projects/claude-code-skills-factory`; line 259 repeats the same path. Developer username `rezarezvani` and local project directory are exposed in the distributed agent file.

Pre-scan matched subprocess.run pattern. Investigation confirms the call uses list-form argument with shell=False (default). No injection surface exists. — subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=300) where cmd is assembled as a list[str] with no shell=True argument. Shell injection requires shell=True; list-form invocation is safe.

11 of 11 slash commands with valid frontmatter omit the `allowed-tools` field. Without this field Claude Code cannot enforce tool boundaries. Affected files: ci-guard, sync-branch, run-release, sync-todos-to-github, review, security-scan, git/rv, git/cp, git/sc, git/cm, git/pr. Representative file shown. — Frontmatter block for ci-guard.md contains description and argument-hint fields but no allowed-tools field. Pattern confirmed across all 11 commands with valid frontmatter.

High-density vague quantifiers across all 14 skill definitions: 'comprehensive', 'intelligent', 'seamless', 'advanced', 'actionable', 'data-driven' used without measurable criteria. Worst offender: tech-stack-evaluator (8 instances). Systematic output of the factory generation template. Representative file shown. — 'comprehensive' appears 6 times, 'intelligent' once, 'data-driven' used as capability descriptor without quantified threshold. Pattern confirmed across all 14 SKILL.md files (average 5–6 vague terms per file).

All 5 agent files use 3–5 vague quantifiers (comprehensive, seamless, effective, thorough) without measurable thresholds. The phrasing adds length without adding precision about what the agent delivers. Representative file shown. — 'comprehensive' appears 3 times, 'seamlessly' once, 'well-structured' once across the agent definition body. Pattern present across all 5 agent files (average 4 vague terms per file).

Uses positional shell variables $1/$2 to parse slash command arguments. Claude Code supplies arguments via $ARGUMENTS only; at runtime $1 and $2 expand to empty strings, silently producing a GitHub issue with an empty title. — Command body uses $1 and $2 for PLAN_TITLE and secondary argument extraction. Claude Code slash commands receive all arguments as a single $ARGUMENTS string; $1 and $2 are not set at runtime.

The generated-skills/CLAUDE.md catalog is out of sync with the repository: 5 confirmed skills are present on disk but absent from the 'Available Skills' section. Users browsing the catalog to discover skills will miss roughly one-third of the available catalog. — generated-skills/CLAUDE.md 'Available Skills' section and 'Skill Size Reference' table list 9 skills. Direct audit of generated-skills/ directory found SKILL.md files for tdd-guide, tech-stack-evaluator, social-media-analyzer, scrum-master-agent, and codex-cli-bridge not present in the catalog.
